title: Lourea obcordata
taxonomy: Lourea obcordata is classified under the family Loasaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the lovage family. The genus Lourea is native to South America and contains several species of flowering plants. Lourea obcordata is a species of flowering plant that is endemic to the Andes mountains in South America. It is a member of the order Ericales, which is a large and diverse order of flowering plants.
uses: Lourea obcordata is reported to have medicinal properties, with local communities using it to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves and bark are often used to treat fever and other infections, as well as for their anti-inflammatory properties.
